title: pouteria cerasifera
intro: Pouteria cerasifera, also known as the Red Ironwood or Red Ebony, is a tropical tree native to the Americas and the Caribbean, characterized by its distinctive red or reddish-brown heartwood. Traditionally, Pouteria cerasifera has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The tree's wood and bark are rich in tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, Pouteria cerasifera is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
